title: AMP leaders defend share price, tout AI future
description: There is no sideline position to take in the disruptive moment offered by artificial intelligence, the leader of an Australian wealth stalwart says.
content:
Wealth management giant AMP has defended a post-earnings share price tumble, instead asking investors to look to its future.

title: TSMC's Q1 revenue jumps 35% y/y, beats market forecasts
description: The world's largest contract chipmaker, TSMC, reported first-quarter revenue of T$1.134 trillion ($35.71 billion) ​on Friday, rising 35% on the year to ‌beat market forecasts on surging interest in artificial intelligence (AI) applications.
content: TAIPEI, April 10 (Reuters) - The world's largest contract chipmaker, TSMC, reported first-quarter revenue of T$1.134 trillion ($35.71 billion) ​on Friday, rising 35% on the year to ‌beat market forecasts on surging interest in artificial intelligence... [1320 chars]

title: OpenAI pauses UK Stargate data centre - a blow to Starmer's AI hub ambitions
description: OpenAI has hit pause on its ambitious UK data centre project, raising fresh concerns over Britain’s ability to sustain large-scale AI infrastructure. With energy costs and regulatory uncertainty in focus, the move could complicate the government’s push to position the UK as a global hub for artificial intelligence innovation.
content:
OpenAI has put the brakes on its proposed UK data centre project, casting a shadow over the government’s ambitions to establish the country as a leading artificial intelligence hub.
The initiative, known as Stargate UK, was envisioned as a key part o... [2773 chars]

title: Chinese startup ShengShu raises $293 million to advance artificial general intelligence
description: Founded in early 2023 by ​Tsinghua University alum Zhu Jun, ShengShu became the first Chinese company to release a video generation model when it launched Vidu in April 2024. The model was positioned as a competitor to OpenAI's Sora, which the US company later discontinued.

title: Wipro shares jump 3% as board eyes buyback after three years on April 16
description: Wipro share price rallied 3% after the IT firm announced it would consider a share buyback. This potential move comes after a significant stock decline this year. The company is also focusing on artificial intelligence as a key growth driver. Management expects some margin pressure but remains confident in maintaining operating margins. Analyst sentiment is cautious regarding the stock.
content: Shares of Wipro, one of India’s leading IT firms, rose as much as 3% to their day's high of Rs 209 on the BSE on Friday after the company said it will consider a share buyback proposal alongside its fourth-quarter results, scheduled for April 16. Thi... [3645 chars]

title: Karnataka to roll out AI-based selfie attendance for staff from May 1
description: BENGALURU: Karnataka govt will replace its biometric attendance system with an artificial intelligence (AI) and GIS-enabled platform from May 1, aimin.
content: BENGALURU: Karnataka govt will replace its biometric attendance system with an artificial intelligence (AI) and GIS-enabled platform from May 1, aiming to boost accountability and simplify logging of daily attendance by employees. The move will cover... [2029 chars]

title: ASA Architect Expo 2026: Global Architects Explore AI and Creativity at ASA International Forum
description: As artificial intelligence reshapes our daily lives, professionals across all sectors are compelled to adapt. The architectural and design community is no exception as it is currently undergoing a transformation to harmonise traditional expertise with cutting-edge technology. This shift seeks a vital balance between human wisdom, creativity, and the innovative power of AI to elevate our economy, society, and overall quality of life.

description: The reorganisation ⁠comes as Meta plans sweeping layoffs that could eliminate tens of ​thousands of jobs at the company, as it seeks to offset costly ​artificial intelligence infrastructure bets and prepare for greater efficiency brought about by AI-assisted workers.

title: Take the judiciary’s cue: Indian courts have achieved clarity on what AI enhances and what it endangers
description: Indian high courts have issued clear instructions barring the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in important judicial processes, while allowing its controlled use for low-risk tasks. Policymakers have much to learn from the judiciary’s approach to this technology.
content:
Judges and court staff are prohibited not only from using AI to decide cases, but also from drafting orders, evaluating evidence or assisting them in judicial reasoning.
The Kerala high court took a similar stance in 2025. India’s Supreme Court has a... [3022 chars]
